title: Calendar
statusLabel: In progress — first improvements live, polish continuing
journey: Having a task assigned to you isn't the same as knowing when you'll actually do it. The calendar turns a pile of assigned work into a realistic day-by-day and week-by-week plan — scheduling tasks into real time, showing what's overlapping or over-committed, and letting staff drag work around as plans change. A basic calendar surface already exists; this feature is the next layer of polish that came directly out of a real testing pass with staff, and the first of those improvements have now landed.

subfeatures.name: Drag-to-schedule feedback
subfeatures.note: A clear ghost preview and no snap-back lag when dragging a task onto the calendar — a first version has landed, with polish still underway.

subfeatures.name: Smart time snapping
subfeatures.note: Dragging snaps to sensible intervals that scale with how fast you're dragging.

subfeatures.name: Side-by-side overlaps
subfeatures.note: Overlapping items sit next to each other instead of stacking illegibly.

subfeatures.name: Condensed daily view
subfeatures.note: Fit a full working day on screen without excessive scrolling.

subfeatures.name: Weekly drag-to-reschedule
subfeatures.note: Change a task's time of day directly from the weekly view.

subfeatures.name: Single-click quick-edit
subfeatures.note: Edit a task's name, time, or date inline, without opening the full task view — flagged by staff as a high-priority fix.

subfeatures.name: Due-time display
subfeatures.note: Show the actual due time, not just the day, on the task view.

subfeatures.name: Drag ideas onto the calendar
subfeatures.note: Drop a captured idea straight onto a day or time slot to turn it into a scheduled task — the original idea is filed away automatically.

testing.fixtureNote: The calendar is now checked against the shared sample-school fixture used across features. Automated tests confirm the daily, weekly, and monthly views render correctly from that fixture, and that dropping an idea onto the calendar creates a scheduled task. Some finer behaviours — such as keeping archived tasks off the calendar and saving a drag reliably — are recorded as known gaps awaiting their own tests.

expectedOutcomes.expected: The daily calendar appears with each task placed by its scheduled time; tasks with only a date are spread across sensible working hours.

expectedOutcomes.criterion: Weekly view shows the full week
expectedOutcomes.expected: Switching to the week view shows all seven day columns, with each task grouped under the right day.

expectedOutcomes.criterion: Month view lays out the programme
expectedOutcomes.expected: The month view renders day-by-day sections with a pill for each scheduled item.

expectedOutcomes.criterion: Dropping an idea creates a task
expectedOutcomes.expected: Dragging an idea from the inbox onto any calendar view creates and places a task there — following the same drag preview rules as moving a task — and files the original idea away.
